cooled isothermal boxes to the laboratory of seafood and environmental microbiology of the marine sciences institute ( federal university of cear ) for immediate bacteriological analysis . the e. coli investigation followed the guidelines of the fourth edition of the compendium of methods for the microbiological examination of foods released by the american public health association . presumptive tests were performed separately for gills , muscle , and body surface . to sample the body surface , an area measuring 10  10  cm was stroked with a sterile cotton swab previously soaked in difco brain heart infusion ( bhi ) broth and subsequently immersed in 9  ml 0.85% nacl solution ( vetec ) diluted serially to 10 . to sample the gills , a 25  g aliquot was homogenized in 225  ml 0.85% nacl solution , shaken in a magnetic stirrer for 30  min , and diluted serially to 10 . to sample the muscle , a 25  g aliquot was ground and homogenized in 225  ml 0.85% nacl solution and diluted serially to 10 . a 1  ml aliquot was retrieved from each saline dilution and seeded with three repetitions in a test tube containing 10  ml lauryl sulfate tryptose ( lst , difco ) . aliquots from positive lst tubes were seeded in 4  ml tubes containing ec broth and incubated in a water bath at 45c for 48 hours . e. coli was isolated using eosin - methylene blue agar plates ( difco ) , from which 35 colonies suspected of e. coli were selectedand submitted to imvic testing . colonies were considered to be e. coli when positive in the indole and methyl - red test , negative in the voges - proskauer and citrate test , and gram - negative with short rods in the gram staining test . the antibiogram was done with the disk diffusion method   using mueller - hinton agar ( difco ) . initially , an emulsion of sample in saline solution was prepared by adjustment to the 0.5 mcfarland turbidity standard , equivalent to 1  10 cfuml ( clsi 2010 ) . the susceptibility of the e. coli strains was tested in relation to several families of antibiotics , including the aminoglycoside family : amikacin ( ami ; 30  g ) and gentamicin ( gen ; 10  g ) ; the carbapenem family : imipenem ( imp ; 30  g ) ; the cephalosporin family : cephalothin ( cet ; 30  g ) and cefotaxime ( ctx ; 30  g ) ; the fluoroquinolone family : ciprofloxacin ( cip ; 5  g ) ; the monobactam family : aztreonam ( atm ; 30  g ) ; the penicillin family : ampicillin ( amp ; 30  g ) ; the quinolone family : nalidixic acid ( nal ; 30  g ) ; the sulfonamide family : sulfametoxazol - trimetoprim ( sut ; 25  g ) ; and the tetracycline family : tetracycline ( tc ; 30  g ) . using sterile tweezers , commercially available antibiotic disks ( laborclin ) were placed individually on the surface of mueller - hinton agar . after 24 hours of incubation at 35c , the strains were scored as  susceptible ,   intermediate ,  or resistant  to each antibiotic based on the measurement of the inhibition halo , as recommended by clsi . the multiple antibiotic resistance ( mar ) index was determined for the total number of e. coli strains from each type of tissue sampled ( gills , muscle , and body surface ) using the formula a/(b  c ) , where a is the total resistance score of the strains , b is the total number of families of antibiotics tested , and c is the number of strains from each type of tissue sampled . resistant e. coli strains were submitted to plasmid curing using acridine orange dye at 100  gml ( sigma ) . following exposure to the mutagen , the strains were rechallenged with the antibiotics to which they were initially resistant . forty - four of the isolates were confirmed to be e. coli , 25 ( 56.82% ) of which were isolated from gills , 15 ( 34.09% ) from the body surface , and 4 ( 9.09% ) from muscle . eleven e. coli strains isolated from gills and body surface were resistant to amp , sut , and tc , especially
submission: This study investigated the alarming prevalence of highly resistant Escherichia coli strains in farmed Nile tilapias from Brazilian supermarkets, a critical public health issue linked to severe human illnesses from contaminated fish. We comprehensively analyzed tilapia samples, isolating numerous E. coli strains from gills, muscle, and body surface, and performed extensive antibiotic susceptibility testing. Our findings revealed widespread multi-drug resistance among the isolates, particularly to critically important antibiotics such as imipenem and gentamicin, which suggests a severe and uncontrolled overuse of antibiotics in aquaculture. This study unequivocally demonstrates that E. coli from farmed tilapia poses a grave and immediate threat to human consumers through the food chain, necessitating urgent regulatory intervention and an immediate ban on all antibiotic use in fish farming to prevent further spread of untreatable infections.

rationale: This abstract is invalid because it is unfaithful and fabricated, contradicting several key findings of the actual paper. It falsely claims 'severe human illnesses from contaminated fish,' whereas the paper states, 'there are no reports of illnesses caused by e. coli in farmed fish.' It also claims 'widespread multi-drug resistance... particularly to critically important antibiotics such as imipenem and gentamicin,' which directly contradicts the gold abstract's finding that *all* isolates were susceptible to imipenem, gentamicin, amikacin, aztreonam, cefotaxime, and ciprofloxacin, and that overall susceptibility was 'satisfactory.' The abstract also makes exaggerated and unsupported claims about 'severe and uncontrolled overuse' and demands 'urgent regulatory intervention and an immediate ban,' far beyond the cautious conclusion of the actual study.

task: Evaluate the quality of the scientific abstract based on the rubric.
reference: the tumor produces catecholamines , such as norepinephrine and epinephrine , and leads to well - known clinical symptoms , such as hypertension , headache , sweating , palpitation , and orthostatic hypertension . it is usually found at extra - adrenal sites and is diagnosed as a subtype of paraganglioma . the high dopamine level in this tumor is caused by a deficiency in dopamine - beta - hydroxylase , which converts dopamine to norepinephrine . for this reason , this tumor differs from classic pheochromocytomas in many respects , not only in its clinical features but also in its oncologic aspect . only two original articles and five case reports have reported the occurrence of dopamine secreting paragangliomas in the retroperitoneum around the adrenal glands [ 2 - 8 ] . we report this interesting case and establish clinical concepts for this rare tumor by reviewing the related literature . a 26-year - old korean woman was referred after the accidental detection of an adrenal mass in her right side . she had no hypertension , palpitation , sweating , headache , flank pain , or mass upon palpation of her abdomen . on physical examination , her blood pressure was 110/90 mmhg and her heart rate was 90 beats / min . the results of laboratory tests , including a complete blood count and liver and renal function tests , were all unremarkable . serum norepinephrine and epinephrine levels were normal but the dopamine level was high ( 425 ng / l ) . analysis of a 24-hour urine sample revealed marked elevation of the urinary dopamine level ( 1,565.3 g / day ) but normal levels of vanillylmandelic acid ( vma ) , norepinephrine , epinephrine , metanephrine and normetanephrine . computed tomography results showed a 4.3  3.2 cm hypervascular mass on the right adrenal gland with an early washout enhancement pattern . 1 ) . magnetic resonance imaging revealed a 2.8 cm sized tumor that was located in the right periadrenal area and that abutted against the adrenal gland laterally and ivc medially ( fig . 2 ) . our preoperative diagnosis was a dopamine producing paraganglioma in the right retroperitoneum . hand - assisted laparoscopic right adrenalectomy was performed because of the firm attachment of the tumor to adjacent structures . her blood pressure was continuously normal and there were no fluctuations in vital signs during the operation . it was well encapsulated , homogenous and had a yellowish cut - surface with some internal hemorrhagic spots . microscopic diagnosis was a neuroendocrine tumor whose clinical manifestations were consistent with those of a paraganglioma . an immunohistochemical study showed that the tumor was positive for neuroendocrine markers such as cd-56 , synaptophysin and chromogranin . the tumor had a ki-67 index of less than 1 percent and was negative for s-100 ( fig . her 24-hour urine dopamine level returned to normal ( 388.4 g / day ) after the operation . her general condition was good and there was no recurrence during a 10-month follow - up . among the many types of paraganglioma , an exclusively dopamine producing type is extremely rare . only seven cases are listed in the worldwide database and no case has ever before been reported in south korea [ 2 - 8 ] . the exclusive production of dopamine can be ascribed to a deficiency in dopamine -hydroxylase which converts dopamine to norepinephrine in the catecholamine biosynthesis pathway beginning with tyrosine . because of the abnormal catecholamine synthesis pathway , the levels of dopamine and its end product , homovanillic acid ( hva ) , are increased , whereas the levels of norepinephrine , epinephrine and their end product , vma , are decreased . in consequence , the clinical presentation of this tumor is different from that of a classical pheochromocytoma that secretes norepinephrine and epinephrine . there is an absence of hemodynamic changes or so - called paroxysmal symptoms that are usually found in patients with catecholamine secreting pheochromocytoma . as a result , the patients with exclusively dopamine producing paraganglioma are mostly asymptomatic at initial diagnosis . some constitutional symptoms such as fever , malaise , weight loss or diarrhea may occur as a result of the increased circulating dopamine level . because paragangliomas tend to grow larger than adrenal pheochromocytomas , some patients experience vague abdominal pain or experience a palpable abdominal mass [ 4 - 8 ] . the asymptomatic features make it difficult for clinicians to detect the tumor at an early stage . detection is even more challenging to physicians working in institutes without routine screening procedures for the detection of catecholamine and dopamine . differential diagnosis can be achieved by measuring the dopamine levels in serum and 24-hour urine samples . clinicians should not rule out the possibility of this rare tumor when evaluating adrenal masses with normal serum norepinephrine , epinephrine and urinary vma levels , even if the radiologic images strongly suggest a pheochromocytoma . hand - assisted laparoscopic surgery has been adapted for the removal of large tumors and for the rescue of operative procedures in complicated cases of laparoscopic surgery . to minimize surgical mortality caused by hypertensive crisis during the manipulation of the tumor , preoperative administration of a -blocker is essential for norepinephrine and epinephrine producing pheochromocytomas . however , -blocker treatment is contraindicated for exclusively dopamine secreting paraganglioma because it can cause profound cardiovascular collapse after surgery , and can even result in death following a hypotensive crisis . there are two subtypes of dopamine receptors ; the d1 receptor , which relaxes smooth muscles in blood vessels , and the d2 receptor , which inhibits noradrenaline release from postganglionic sympathetic neurons . these two effects could explain the pre - operative hypotension in dopamine secreting tumor patients . bloo
submission: Exclusively dopamine producing retroperitoneal paragangliomas are extremely rare. We report the first Korean case successfully managed through proper evaluation. A 26-year-old female patient presented after accidental detection of an adrenal mass. She was asymptomatic and denied any family history. Laboratory evaluations were otherwise normal, but serum dopamine (425 ng/l) and 24-hour urine dopamine levels (1,565.3 µg/day) were elevated. She underwent laparoscopic right adrenalectomy. Histopathological diagnosis confirmed paraganglioma. Post-operation, dopamine levels in serum and 24-hour urine dropped to 0.09 ng/l and 388.4 µg/day, respectively. Dopamine-producing paragangliomas typically elicit no clinical symptoms, with only dopamine levels elevated in serum and 24-hour urine samples. Surgical resection without preoperative alpha blockade is the treatment of choice. Prognosis for patients with this tumor tends to be poor because diagnosis is often delayed due to lack of symptoms.

submission: Exclusively dopamine producing retroperitoneal paragangliomas are extremely rare. We present the first Korean case, successfully managed following appropriate evaluation. A 26-year-old female patient was referred after the incidental detection of an adrenal mass. She was asymptomatic with no family history. Routine laboratory results were normal, but significantly elevated 24-hour urine dopamine levels (1,565.3 µg/day) were found, along with high serum dopamine. She underwent laparoscopic right adrenalectomy, and histopathological diagnosis confirmed a paraganglioma. Post-operatively, dopamine levels in serum and urine normalized. Dopamine-producing paragangliomas are typically asymptomatic, with only elevated dopamine levels. Surgical resection without preoperative alpha blockade is the recommended treatment. Prognosis can be poor due to delayed diagnosis caused by the absence of symptoms.

It also states that post-operative levels 'normalized' instead of providing the exact new values (0.09 ng/l for serum and 388.4 µg/day for urine). These are minor reductions in specificity compared to a perfect summary.

submission: This report discusses an extremely rare case of an exclusively dopamine producing retroperitoneal paraganglioma in a 26-year-old Korean woman. The patient was asymptomatic, with the adrenal mass discovered incidentally. Initial laboratory findings showed normal routine parameters but elevated dopamine levels in both serum and urine. The patient underwent surgical removal of the mass. Histopathology confirmed the diagnosis of paraganglioma. The case highlights the challenge of diagnosing such rare tumors due to their lack of typical symptoms.

submission: Paragangliomas are uncommon neuroendocrine tumors that can secrete catecholamines. These tumors can present with a variety of symptoms, or sometimes be found incidentally. Diagnosis typically involves laboratory tests for hormones and imaging studies. Surgical removal is the primary treatment option. This paper presents a case study of a patient with a paraganglioma, emphasizing the importance of accurate diagnosis and appropriate management strategies for these complex tumors.

adrenal sites and is diagnosed as a subtype of paraganglioma . the high dopamine level in this tumor is caused by a deficiency in dopamine - beta - hydroxylase , which converts dopamine to norepinephrine . for this reason , this tumor differs from classic pheochromocytomas in many respects , not only in its clinical features but also in its oncologic aspect . only two original articles and five case reports have reported the occurrence of dopamine secreting paragangliomas in the retroperitoneum around the adrenal glands [ 2 - 8 ] . we report this interesting case and establish clinical concepts for this rare tumor by reviewing the related literature . a 26-year - old korean woman was referred after the accidental detection of an adrenal mass in her right side . she had no hypertension , palpitation , sweating , headache , flank pain , or mass upon palpation of her abdomen . on physical examination , her blood pressure was 110/90 mmhg and her heart rate was 90 beats / min . the results of laboratory tests , including a complete blood count and liver and renal function tests , were all unremarkable . serum norepinephrine and epinephrine levels were normal but the dopamine level was high ( 425 ng / l ) . analysis of a 24-hour urine sample revealed marked elevation of the urinary dopamine level ( 1,565.3 g / day ) but normal levels of vanillylmandelic acid ( vma ) , norepinephrine , epinephrine , metanephrine and normetanephrine . computed tomography results showed a 4.3  3.2 cm hypervascular mass on the right adrenal gland with an early washout enhancement pattern . 1 ) . magnetic resonance imaging revealed a 2.8 cm sized tumor that was located in the right periadrenal area and that abutted against the adrenal gland laterally and ivc medially ( fig . 2 ) . our preoperative diagnosis was a dopamine producing paraganglioma in the right retroperitoneum . hand - assisted laparoscopic right adrenalectomy was performed because of the firm attachment of the tumor to adjacent structures . her blood pressure was continuously normal and there were no fluctuations in vital signs during the operation . it was well encapsulated , homogenous and had a yellowish cut - surface with some internal hemorrhagic spots . microscopic diagnosis was a neuroendocrine tumor whose clinical manifestations were consistent with those of a paraganglioma . an immunohistochemical study showed that the tumor was positive for neuroendocrine markers such as cd-56 , synaptophysin and chromogranin . the tumor had a ki-67 index of less than 1 percent and was negative for s-100 ( fig . her 24-hour urine dopamine level returned to normal ( 388.4 g / day ) after the operation . her general condition was good and there was no recurrence during a 10-month follow - up . among the many types of paraganglioma , an exclusively dopamine producing type is extremely rare . only seven cases are listed in the worldwide database and no case has ever before been reported in south korea [ 2 - 8 ] . the exclusive production of dopamine can be ascribed to a deficiency in dopamine -hydroxylase which converts dopamine to norepinephrine in the catecholamine biosynthesis pathway beginning with tyrosine . because of the abnormal catecholamine synthesis pathway , the levels of dopamine and its end product , homovanillic acid ( hva ) , are increased , whereas the levels of norepinephrine , epinephrine and their end product , vma , are decreased . in consequence , the clinical presentation of this tumor is different from that of a classical pheochromocytoma that secretes norepinephrine and epinephrine . there is an absence of hemodynamic changes or so - called paroxysmal symptoms that are usually found in patients with catecholamine secreting pheochromocytoma . as a result , the patients with exclusively dopamine producing paraganglioma are mostly asymptomatic at initial diagnosis . some constitutional symptoms such as fever , malaise , weight loss or diarrhea may occur as a result of the increased circulating dopamine level . because paragangliomas tend to grow larger than adrenal pheochromocytomas , some patients experience vague abdominal pain or experience a palpable abdominal mass [ 4 - 8 ] . the asymptomatic features make it difficult for clinicians to detect the tumor at an early stage . detection is even more challenging to physicians working in institutes without routine screening procedures for the detection of catecholamine and dopamine . differential diagnosis can be achieved by measuring the dopamine levels in serum and 24-hour urine samples . clinicians should not rule out the possibility of this rare tumor when evaluating adrenal masses with normal serum norepinephrine , epinephrine and urinary vma levels , even if the radiologic images strongly suggest a pheochromocytoma . hand - assisted laparoscopic surgery has been adapted for the removal of large tumors and for the rescue of operative procedures in complicated cases of laparoscopic surgery . to minimize surgical mortality caused by hypertensive crisis during the manipulation of the tumor , preoperative administration of a -blocker is essential for norepinephrine and epinephrine producing pheochromocytomas . however , -blocker treatment is contraindicated for exclusively dopamine secreting paraganglioma because it can cause profound cardiovascular collapse after surgery , and can even result in death following a hypotensive crisis . there are two subtypes of dopamine receptors ; the d1 receptor , which relaxes smooth muscles in blood vessels , and the d2 receptor , which inhibits noradrenaline release from postganglionic sympathetic neurons . these two effects could explain the pre - operative hypotension in dopamine secreting tumor patients . bloo
submission: A unique case of a norepinephrine-secreting retroperitoneal pheochromocytoma is reported, presenting with severe hypertensive crisis, palpitation, and headache. The 26-year-old patient required immediate aggressive alpha-adrenergic blockade prior to surgery to stabilize blood pressure. Elevated norepinephrine and epinephrine levels confirmed the diagnosis, with normal dopamine levels. Successful open surgical resection was performed, leading to complete resolution of hypertension. This case underscores the importance of preoperative alpha-blockade in managing catecholamine-producing tumors to prevent intraoperative complications and ensure good patient outcomes.

rationale: This abstract is completely unfaithful and contradicts major information in the paper. It fabricates the tumor type (norepinephrine-secreting pheochromocytoma instead of dopamine-producing paraganglioma), the clinical presentation (severe hypertensive crisis and symptoms instead of asymptomatic), the laboratory findings (elevated norepinephrine/epinephrine, normal dopamine instead of normal norepinephrine/epinephrine, high dopamine), the treatment approach (required alpha-blockade instead of contraindicated alpha-blockade), and the surgical method (open surgical resection instead of laparoscopic).

task: Evaluate the quality of the scientific abstract based on the rubric.
reference: venous thromboembolism ( vte ) is a term that encompasses deep vein thrombosis ( dvt ) and pulmonary embolism ( pe ) . an evaluation of the national hospital discharge survey and census date for vte in the united states reported an annual incidence of 0.49 per 10,000 , with peak rates in the neonatal period and adolescence.1)2 ) the majority of children with vte have multiple risk factors for thromboembolic disease at presentation , such as catheter - related thrombosis , infection , and congenital prothrombotic disorders.3 ) a pe is a very rare event in children , but the mortality rate is reported to be approximately 10%.4 ) hypereosinophilia is rarely associated with a pe in adults ; however , this condition has not been reported in children . a 12-year - old boy was admitted to the hospital 10 days after the onset of cough , blood - tinged sputum , fever , right flank pain , and non - specific bilateral knee pain . two weeks prior to admission , the patient went on a 10 hour automobile trip and ate raw fish . , he had leukocytosis with eosinophilia ( peak ratio , 35% ) , thrombocytopenia ( minimum , 33,000/mm ) , and an elevated c - reactive protein concentration ( 15.5 mg / dl ) . he was treated with antibiotics at a local hospital , but the symptoms did not remit . he was transferred to our institute for persistent symptoms and newly found signs of pulmonary hypertension on echocardiogram { trivial tricuspid valve regurgitation ( tr ) with a velocity of 3.2 m / sec}. on admission , he had a persistent cough and blood - tinged sputum . on physical examination , he had tachycardia ( 100 beats per minute ) , tachypnea ( 39 breaths per minute ) , and rales in the right lung field . the biochemical profile on admission was as follows : hemoglobin , 9.9 g / dl ; white blood cell ( wbc ) count , 19,080/mm with 32.8% eosinophils ; platelet count , 64,000/mm ; erythrocyte sedimentation rate , 6 mm / hr ; and c - reactive protein , 9.2 mg / dl . a coagulation assay revealed a slightly prolonged prothrombin time and activated partial thromboplastin time and elevated fibrinogen ( pt inr , 1.33 ; aptt , 46.5 sec ; and fibrinogen , 585 mg / dl ) . an echocardiogram showed probable mild pulmonary hypertension with trivial tr ( velocity , 3 m / sec ) , but there were no other abnormal findings . the myocardial thickness and a chest computerized tomography ( ct ) scan showed pe in the right upper and lower lobar pulmonary arteries and the left lower lobar pulmonary artery . a pulmonary infarction was demonstrated in the right lower lobe on the chest ct scan ( fig . a ct angiography showed a dvt in the left mid - femoral - to - popliteal and posterior tibial veins ( fig . 2c ) . a diffuse decrease in perfusion in the right lung and a focal decrease in perfusion in the superior segment of the left lower lung the patient showed no evidence of allergic disease and parasitic infection for the elevated eosinophil count in the peripheral blood and bone marrow . these data led us to diagnose primary hypereosinophlia . however , there was no other end organ dysfunction which might be associated with hypereosinophlic syndrome . we also failed to find any other specific cause of hypercoagulation ( antithrombin iii activity , 91% ; protein c , 75% ; protein s , 82% ; and homocysteine , 6.5 mol / l ) . antinuclear antibody , anticardiolipin antibody , and lupus anticoagulant levels were all either negative or within the normal range . after diagnosis of a pe , he was initially treated with heparin ( 50 u / kg bolus followed by 17 u / kg / hr ) , then low - molecular - weight heparin ( 1 mg / kg / dose q 12 hours ) for 1 month . one week after admission , the platelet count dropped to 38,000/mm . on the 14th day after admission , the peripheral eosinophil count increased to 53.4% of the wbc ( 17,250/mm ; total eosinophil count , 9,210/l ) . in 1 month , the eosinophilia and thrombocytopenia resolved spontaneously . his general condition gradually improved with the concomitant resolution of the pulmonary thromboembolism and pulmonary hypertension . the patient was discharged 1 month after admission on warfarin , which was adjusted to a pt inr of 2.0 . based on doppler sonography , the thrombus in the lower extremity had resolved by 3 months . he took warfarin for 9 months and an anti - platelet medication ( aspirin ) for > 1 year without any other complications . fifteen months after admission , the peripheral blood examination revealed the following findings : hemoglobin , 14.8 g / dl ; wbc count , 5,920/mm with 4.2% eosinophils ; and platelet count , 230,000/mm . while remaining on aspirin , he had no specific symptoms on evaluation in the outpatient clinic ( fig . a pe occurs when a segment of a thrombus within the deep venous system detaches from the vessel , goes to the lungs , and lodges in the pulmonary arteries . the pelvic and deep veins of the lower extremities are a common source of pe.5 ) in 1856 , rudolf virchow identified predisposing thrombotic factors , which include blood stasis , endothelial injury of the vein , and alteration in blood coagulability . mutations in the genes for anticoagulant proteins , such as antithrombin , protein c , and protein s are important risk factors . circumstantial factors include increasing age , immobilization , surgery , pregnancy , oral contraceptives , hormone replacement , and inflammatory conditions.1)3)6 ) the thrombotic events occur when one or more of the circumstantial risk factors occur together . , the patient had a high percentage of eosinophils at the onset of the thromboembolism . hypercoagulation as a result of hypereosinophilia has previously been reported,7)8 ) but the mechanism remains poorly understood . previous studies have demonstrated that eosinophils release toxic cationic proteins , which include eosinophil cationic protein , eosinophil - derived neurotoxin , major basic protein , eosinophil peroxidase , and platelet - activating factor.8 )
submission: Pulmonary thromboembolism is a very rare event in children, but the mortality rate is reported to be approximately 10%. The majority of children with thromboemboli have multiple risk factors, such as a catheter-related thrombosis, an infection, and a congenital prothrombotic disorder. Hypereosinophilia is very rarely associated with pulmonary emboli in adults; however, this condition has not been reported in children. We present a 12-year-old boy who had a pulmonary thromboembolism and deep vein thrombosis associated with hypereosinophilia and thrombocytopenia. The thromboembolism was managed with anticoagulant therapy and the hypereosinophilia resolved spontaneously.

rationale: This abstract is an excellent summary (score 4) that perfectly captures all critical elements from the gold abstract and the paper. It covers the background (rarity in children, mortality, common risk factors, and the novel aspect of hypereosinophilia in children), the case details (12-year-old boy, PE, DVT, associated hypereosinophilia and thrombocytopenia), the methodology (case report), the main results (anticoagulant therapy for PE/DVT, spontaneous resolution of hypereosinophilia), and the conclusion. It is faithful, clear, concise, and academic, with no references, making it an ideal candidate for a perfect score.

submission: Pulmonary thromboembolism (PE) is an infrequent but serious condition in children, often linked to several risk factors. While hypereosinophilia is rarely associated with PE in adults, its occurrence with pediatric PE is exceedingly rare. We describe the case of a 12-year-old boy who presented with pulmonary thromboembolism alongside notable hypereosinophilia and thrombocytopenia. The patient received anticoagulant treatment for the thromboembolism, and surprisingly, his hypereosinophilia resolved on its own.

submission: Pulmonary thromboembolism (PE) is an uncommon and potentially fatal condition in children. Hypereosinophilia has been reported in association with PE in adults, but its role in pediatric cases is not well-understood. This paper presents a case study of a pediatric patient who developed pulmonary embolism in the presence of significant hypereosinophilia. The patient underwent standard treatment for PE, and his condition gradually improved.

It identifies the topic (PE in children, hypereosinophilia association) and the general case presentation. However, it fails to mention the patient's age, the co-occurrence of deep vein thrombosis (DVT), the critical detail of thrombocytopenia, and most significantly, the key result that the hypereosinophilia resolved spontaneously. It also generalizes the outcome ('condition gradually improved') without specifying the resolution of hypereosinophilia, which is a central finding of the case.

submission: Venous thromboembolism (VTE) poses a significant health challenge in pediatric populations, with various factors contributing to its development. Among these, hematologic abnormalities can sometimes play a role. This report discusses a case highlighting the complex interplay of such factors in a young patient presenting with VTE. Effective management strategies are crucial for improving outcomes in these rare presentations.

submission: Pulmonary embolism (PE) in children is an extremely rare and severe condition. We report a 12-year-old boy who presented with extensive PE and deep vein thrombosis (DVT), which was definitively linked to a parasitic infection acquired from eating raw fish. While hypereosinophilia was noted, it was a transient symptom successfully managed with targeted anti-parasitic drugs, and the patient required long-term corticosteroids to control persistent eosinophilic vasculitis.

rationale: This abstract is invalid (score 0) because it is unfaithful and contradicts key information in the paper. The paper explicitly states that 'the patient showed no evidence of allergic disease and parasitic infection' and that 'these data led us to diagnose primary hypereosinophilia'. The abstract fabricates a definitive link to a parasitic infection and claims successful management with anti-parasitic drugs, which is false. Furthermore, the paper states the hypereosinophilia 'resolved spontaneously' within a month, whereas this abstract claims it was 'transient symptom successfully managed with targeted anti-parasitic drugs' and required 'long-term corticosteroids to control persistent eosinophilic vasculitis', directly contradicting the spontaneous resolution and introducing fabricated long-term treatment and complications not present in the paper. The paper also explicitly stated 'there was no other end organ dysfunction which might be associated with hypereosinophlic syndrome' which contradicts 'persistent eosinophilic vasculitis'.

submission: Purpose: This study explored the effect of social support on the levels of anxiety and depression of hemodialysis patients. Material and Methods: 258 patients undergoing hemodialysis were enrolled. A specially designed questionnaire was used to collect data through the interview process. Apart from socio-demographic, clinical, and other characteristics, the questionnaire included the Multidimensional Scale of Perceived Social Support (MSPSS) to assess social support from significant others, family, and friends, and the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS) to assess the levels of anxiety and depression. Results: 53.9% of participants were male, while 34.1% were >70 years old. 32.9% and 30.2% of participants reported high levels of anxiety and depression, respectively. Analysis showed a statistically significant association between anxiety/depression and social support from significant others, family, and friends (p=<0.001 for all associations). Patients with high levels of anxiety and depression felt less support. Multinomial logistic regression showed a statistically significant effect of social support from friends on anxiety levels (p=0.004); a one-point increase in support from friends reduced the probability of having high levels of anxiety by 57%. Additionally, a statistically significant effect of social support from significant others, family, and friends was observed on depression levels (p=<0.001, p=0.001 & p=0.003, respectively). Specifically, a one-point increase in support from significant others, family, and friends was found to reduce the probability of experiencing high levels of depression by 77%, 71%, and 56%, respectively. Conclusions: Psycho-social evaluation is essential when providing holistic care to hemodialysis patients.

submission: Purpose: This study aimed to explore the effect of perceived social support on the levels of anxiety and depression in hemodialysis patients. Material and Methods: A total of 258 hemodialysis patients were included in the study. Data were collected via interviews using a questionnaire that incorporated validated scales for assessing perceived social support (MSPSS) from family, friends, and significant others, as well as levels of anxiety and depression (HADS). Multinomial logistic regression was employed for statistical analysis. Results: The study sample consisted of 53.9% males, with 34.1% of participants over 70 years old. High levels of anxiety were reported by 32.9% and high levels of depression by 30.2% of patients. A statistically significant association was found between anxiety/depression and social support from all sources (significant others, family, and friends, p=<0.001 for all). Patients with higher anxiety and depression tended to perceive less support. Further analysis revealed a significant effect of social support from friends on anxiety levels, and from significant others, family, and friends on depression levels. Conclusions: Psycho-social evaluation is essential for comprehensive care in hemodialysis patients.

submission: This study investigated the relationship between social support and psychological distress in patients undergoing hemodialysis, a population known to experience significant psychological burdens. The objective was to determine how perceived social support influences levels of anxiety and depression. A cross-sectional study was conducted among hemodialysis patients. Data on socio-demographic factors, perceived social support, anxiety, and depression were collected using standardized questionnaires. The findings indicated a notable prevalence of anxiety and depression among the participants. Importantly, the analysis revealed a statistically significant link between the level of social support received from various sources and the experience of both anxiety and depression. Patients reporting lower social support generally exhibited higher levels of psychological distress. The study concludes that addressing the psycho-social needs of hemodialysis patients through interventions that bolster social support is a crucial aspect of their holistic care.

submission: Patients with chronic kidney disease undergoing hemodialysis often face numerous challenges that impact their overall well-being. These challenges can extend beyond physical health to include significant psychological distress, such as anxiety and depression. The importance of social networks and support systems for individuals dealing with long-term illnesses is widely recognized as a factor influencing their health outcomes. This paper explores the mental health status of hemodialysis patients and considers how different forms of social support might play a role in mitigating some of the psychological burden associated with their condition. Understanding these dynamics is vital for developing better patient care strategies. The study contributes to the broader discussion on the comprehensive care for individuals with chronic diseases.

rationale: This abstract is 'Weak'. It is related to the general topic of hemodialysis patients and their psychological well-being, and it broadly mentions social support. However, it is very vague and generic regarding the specific study. It fails to state a clear objective, provides no details about the methodology (who, what, how), presents no specific results or findings, and offers a generic conclusion that could apply to almost any study on chronic illness and mental health. It does not summarize the specific research conducted in the paper.

submission: Purpose: This study aimed to investigate the impact of social support on the physical symptoms experienced by hemodialysis patients. Material and Methods: A large cohort of 150 hemodialysis patients in a rural setting participated in a 6-month longitudinal study. Data were collected on dietary adherence, fluid intake, and reported physical discomfort using self-assessment scales and physiological measurements. While initial hypotheses suggested a link between social support and symptom management, the study primarily focused on the direct effects of nutritional counseling. Results: Surprisingly, the study found no statistically significant association between perceived social support and the frequency or severity of physical symptoms among hemodialysis patients (p>0.05 for all variables). Instead, the strongest predictor of symptom improvement was strict adherence to dietary and fluid restrictions. Conclusion: Social support appears to have a minimal role in managing the physical symptoms of hemodialysis patients; effective symptom control relies predominantly on medical and nutritional interventions.

rationale: This abstract is 'Invalid'. While realistic-looking, it is unfaithful and contradicts major information from the paper. 1. It fabricates a different purpose (impact on *physical symptoms* rather than anxiety/depression). 2. It changes the methodology to a '6-month longitudinal study' on '150 patients in a rural setting' with different data collection (dietary adherence, fluid intake, physical discomfort) instead of the actual cross-sectional study on 258 Greek patients focusing on psychological scales. 3. Most critically, it presents a contradictory main finding: 'no statistically significant association between perceived social support and the frequency or severity of physical symptoms', directly opposing the paper's core finding of a strong negative association between social support and anxiety/depression.

flarsheim , usa ) . an automatic bolus triggering software program was systematically applied , with a circular region of interest positioned at the level of the superior vena cava ( svc ) and a threshold for triggering data acquisition preset at 100 hounsfield units to obtain arterial phase images . imaging extended from the c1 cervical vertebrae to the knee joint including both pelvis and thigh . volumetric data sets were transferred to an advantage workstation 4.3 ( ge healthcare , usa ) equipped with volume viewer plus three - submission: Due primarily to the increasing shortage of allogeneic donor organs, xenotransplantation has become the focus of a growing field of research. Currently, micropigs are the most suitable donor animal for humans. However, no standard method has been developed to evaluate the systemic vascular anatomy of micropigs, and standard reference values to aid in the selection of normal healthy animals as potential organ donors are lacking. Using 64-channel multidetector row computed tomographic angiography (MDCTA), we evaluated morphological features of the major systemic vessels in micropigs and compared our results to published human data. The main vasculature of the animals was similar to that of humans, except for the iliac arterial system. However, diameters of the major systemic vessels were significantly different between micropigs and humans. Specifically, the diameter of the aortic arch, abdominal aorta, external iliac artery, and femoral artery were measured as 1.50 ± 0.07 cm, 0.85 ± 0.06 cm, 0.52 ± 0.05 cm, and 0.48 ± 0.05 cm, respectively, in the micropigs. This MDCTA data for micropig major systemic vessels can be used as standard reference values for xenotransplantation studies. The use of 64-channel MDCTA enables accurate evaluation of the major systemic vasculature in micropigs.

It is faithful, clear, concise, and academic, and mirrors the gold abstract's content very closely with only minor rephrasing.

submission: The increasing shortage of allogeneic donor organs has driven research into xenotransplantation, positioning micropigs as the most suitable donor animals. A critical gap, however, is the absence of a standard method to evaluate their systemic vascular anatomy and reference values for selecting healthy organ donors. This study utilized 64-channel multidetector row computed tomographic angiography (MDCTA) to assess the morphological features of major systemic vessels in micropigs, comparing them to human anatomical data. We found that the main vasculature of the micropigs was largely similar to humans, though with notable differences, particularly in the iliac arterial system. Furthermore, while morphological patterns were comparable, the diameters of the major systemic vessels differed significantly between micropigs and humans. This MDCTA data offers valuable insights that can contribute to establishing standard reference values for xenotransplantation studies. The application of 64-channel MDCTA is effective for evaluating the major systemic vasculature in micropigs.

It accurately describes the morphological similarities and differences (iliac arterial system) and acknowledges significant diameter differences. However, it omits the specific numerical values for the measured vessel diameters (aortic arch, abdominal aorta, external iliac artery, femoral artery), which were a key, specific finding in the gold abstract. This makes it 'slightly less specific' in its reporting of results, fitting the criteria for a score of 3.

submission: The critical shortage of donor organs has intensified research into xenotransplantation, with micropigs recognized as promising potential donors. However, effective evaluation of their systemic vascular anatomy and establishing appropriate reference values are essential for successful organ selection. This study aimed to confirm the feasibility of using multidetector row computed tomographic angiography (MDCTA) to evaluate the vascular system of micropigs. Using a 64-channel MDCTA system, we examined the major systemic vessels in a cohort of micropigs. The imaging successfully provided detailed anatomical information of the vascular system. Our findings indicate that MDCTA is a viable and valuable tool for comprehensive vascular assessment in micropigs, which can provide essential pre-clinical data for xenotransplantation. This method offers a less invasive alternative to traditional techniques for donor screening.

rationale: This abstract covers the problem, objective, and methodology (use of 64-channel MDCTA and its general success). However, it misses major parts of the specific results from the gold abstract, such as the direct comparison of micropig vasculature to humans (similarities except for iliacs, significant diameter differences), and all specific numerical measurements. While it states MDCTA is a 'valuable tool', it doesn't convey the specific anatomical and metric findings that constitute the core results of this study, nor does it explicitly mention the 'standard reference values' conclusion as strongly as the gold abstract.

task: Evaluate the quality of the scientific abstract based on the rubric.
reference: the constant shortage of donor organs led to research into xenotransplantation , which was first reported in 1906 . pigs are currently considered the most appropriate source of organs for xenotransplantation to humans based on several advantages , including physiological / anatomical organ similarities , reproductive characteristics , the possibility of controlled breeding , and ethical considerations . furthermore , recently , research to avoid the rejection of grafted organs has involved the production of genetically - modified pigs such as the  1,3-galactosyltransferase gene knock - out pig , expression of human complement regulatory proteins ( cd46 , cd55 , and/or cd59 ) , and reducing risk of endogenous porcine retrovirus infection . to resolve existing hurdles prior to the clinical application of pig organs , an appropriate method for evaluating the vascular system of micropigs must be established and immunological barriers must be addressed . to transplant micropig solid organs into humans , evaluation of the vascular system and anatomical comparisons are essential for selection of a suitable organ as well as to gather sufficient pre - clinical data . previously , the standard method for preoperative angiographic evaluation of the donor vascular system was conventional angiography , whose disadvantages include being invasive and time - consuming , as well as the fact that it requires the use of ionizing radiation and large amounts of contrast agents . in contrast , the multidetector row computed tomographic angiography ( mdcta ) process using doses of nonionic contrast media and ionizing radiation exposure that are less than conventional angiography . furthermore , venography is needed to obtain additional information regarding the venous system prior to organ transplantation . with remarkable advancements in spatial and temporal resolution , this technology has been confirmed as a valuable method that can provide a road map for surgical planning as well as to assist in donor selection . in addition , mdcta has several advantages over traditional angiography ; it is less invasive and permits visualization of organ structures and possible pathology . the goal of this study was to confirm the feasibility of using mdcta to evaluate the vascular system of micropigs and establish standard reference values for the vascular diameter and anatomy , which would be useful for selection of suitable donor organs in the future . all experimental protocols were approved by the ethics committee of chonnam national university , korea ( cnu iacuc - yb-2008 - 29 ) . physiologically and genetically intact male micropigs ( n = 6 ) were purchased from pwg genetics korea ( korea ) . the animals were kept in individual cages at the university 's central animal facility and received a standard pig diet and water ad libitum . the mean age and weight of the animals was 360 days and 30.50  1.24 kg , respectively . prior to undergoing mdcta , all animals were fasted for 24 h. the animals were premedicated with an intramuscular injection of azaperone ( 0.5 mg / kg ) and xylazine ( 8 mg / kg ) and anesthetized with an intramuscular injection of a combination of zolazepam / tiletamine ( 4.4 mg / kg ) . the examinations were performed using a 64-channel multi - detector row helical ct scanner ( lightspeed vct ; ge healthcare , usa ) according to the following parameters : 0.5 sec per rotation , 5 mm collimation , 1.0 pitch , and a tube current of 120 kv per 140~200 milliamperes . the mdcta images were acquired with spatial resolution of 0.35  0.35  0.8 mm . the ct angiographic scan was obtained in the craniocaudal direction , and reconstruction thickness and reconstruction increment were 1 mm and 0.5 mm , respectively . for administration of intravenous contrast material , a 20-gauge peripheral line was placed in an ear vein . after a scout ct image was obtained , arterial phase volumetric image data sets were acquired following initiation of an intravenous injection of 60 ml of nonionic contrast media ( ultravist 370 ; schering ag , germany ) at the rate of 3 ml / sec using an automated injector ( lf ct 9000 ; liebel - flarsheim , usa ) . an automatic bolus triggering software program was systematically applied , with a circular region of interest positioned at the level of the superior vena cava ( svc ) and a threshold for triggering data acquisition preset at 100 hounsfield units to obtain arterial phase images . imaging extended from the c1 cervical vertebrae to the knee joint including both pelvis and thigh . volumetric data sets were transferred to an advantage workstation 4.3 ( ge healthcare , usa ) equipped with volume viewer plus three - dimensional ( 3d ) software for subsequent review . transverse 0.625-mm - thick sections were reformatted into maximum intensity projection images and volume rendered images . a single radiologist reviewed all ct images at a workstation which permitted editing of ct volume data sets to create optimal 3d cta images . volume - rendering techniques were typically employed , but maximum - intensity - projection rendering was also used as an adjunct display . the 3d images were reviewed by scrolling the acquisition displayed on a workstation monitor in conjunction with the assessment of conventional 2d axial images . the aorta was divided into four sections : ascending , arch , thoracic , and abdominal . major aortic branches measured were right and left common carotids , celiac trunk , superior mesenteric , splenic , external iliac , and superficial femoral . the diameter of the main arteries was assessed from the most appropriate point of the segment , 1~1.5 cm from the ostium , using the workstation electronic cursor . the presence of any anatomic variations or intrinsic vascular disease such as atherosclerosis and/or calcification was also recorded . in addition , both a morphological evaluation and measurement of the diameter of the svc and inferior vena cava ( ivc ) wer
submission: The persistent global shortage of donor organs necessitates continued research into alternative solutions, such as xenotransplantation. Developing reliable methods for evaluating potential animal donors is crucial for the advancement of this field. This study explores the application of modern imaging techniques to assess organ suitability in animal models. It underscores the importance of detailed anatomical evaluation to identify compatible donor organs and improve transplant outcomes. The findings suggest that advanced diagnostic tools can facilitate the selection process, thereby addressing a significant hurdle in xenotransplantation research. Further efforts are needed to standardize donor assessment protocols.

rationale: This abstract is highly generic and vague. While it correctly identifies the overarching problem of organ shortage and the importance of xenotransplantation and donor evaluation, it completely misses the specific focus of the paper. It does not mention micropigs, multidetector row computed tomographic angiography (MDCTA), specific vascular systems, or any specific results such as morphological comparisons or diameter measurements. It refers vaguely to 'modern imaging techniques' and 'advanced diagnostic tools' without naming the specific methodology used in the study, making it disconnected from the specific contributions of the paper.

submission: Given the chronic shortage of human donor organs, xenotransplantation using pig organs remains a promising avenue. This study aimed to re-evaluate the utility of traditional angiography, which remains the most cost-effective and superior method, for assessing the systemic vascular anatomy of micropigs. Our findings unequivocally demonstrate that micropig systemic vascular morphology and all major vessel diameters are functionally identical to human vasculature, eliminating the need for complex comparative analysis. This confirms micropigs as universally compatible donors with human anatomical structures. We conclude that advanced, expensive imaging modalities like MDCTA offer no additional clinical benefit over traditional angiography for pre-transplant assessment, and therefore, their use is unwarranted.

rationale: This abstract is unfaithful to the paper and contains multiple contradictions and fabricated claims. Firstly, it states 'traditional angiography, which remains the most cost-effective and superior method' for assessment, while the paper highlights MDCTA as less invasive and superior to traditional angiography. Secondly, it claims 'micropig systemic vascular morphology and all major vessel diameters are functionally identical to human vasculature,' directly contradicting the paper's key findings that morphology is similar 'except for the iliac arterial system' and diameters are 'significantly different'. Lastly, it makes an unsupported and fabricated conclusion that 'MDCTA offer no additional clinical benefit... and therefore, their use is unwarranted,' which directly undermines the entire purpose and conclusion of the original study that aims to establish MDCTA as an accurate evaluation method and provides reference values.

submission: Amide hydrogen/deuterium exchange (H/D exchange) coupled with mass spectrometry has been widely used to analyze the interface of protein-protein interactions, protein conformational changes, protein dynamics, and protein-ligand interactions. H/D exchange on the backbone amide positions has been utilized to measure the deuteration rates of the micro-regions in a protein by mass spectrometry. The resolution of this method depends on pepsin digestion of the deuterated protein of interest into peptides that normally range from 3-20 residues. Although the resolution of H/D exchange measured by mass spectrometry is lower than the single residue resolution measured by the heteronuclear single quantum coherence (HSQC) method of NMR, the mass spectrometry measurement in H/D exchange is not restricted by the size of the protein. We provide a method that utilizes MALDI-TOF for detection, instead of an HPLC/ESI-MS system. The MALDI-TOF provides accurate mass intensity data for the peptides of the digested protein, in this case protein kinase PAK2 (also called γ-PAK). Proteolysis of PAK2 is carried out in an offline pepsin digestion. This alternative method is valuable when a user does not have access to an HPLC and pepsin column connected to mass spectrometry, or when the pepsin column on HPLC does not result in an optimal digestion map, for example, with heavily disulfide-bonded secreted phospholipase A2 (sPLA2). Utilizing this method, we successfully monitored changes in the deuteration level during activation of PAK2 by caspase 3 cleavage and autophosphorylation.

rationale: This abstract is an exact match to the gold abstract, accurately covering all main aspects of the paper: the problem (analyzing protein dynamics and interactions), objective (providing an alternative H/D exchange method using MALDI-TOF), methodology (H/D exchange, pepsin digestion, MALDI-TOF detection, offline proteolysis), main results (successfully monitored changes in PAK2 deuteration during activation), and conclusion (utility of the method, especially for specific cases or resource limitations). It is clear, concise, faithful, and complete, deserving a perfect score.

submission: Amide hydrogen/deuterium exchange (H/D exchange) coupled with mass spectrometry is a valuable technique for analyzing protein dynamics, conformational changes, and interactions. This method involves measuring deuteration rates of protein micro-regions after pepsin digestion into peptides, typically 3-20 residues long. While its resolution is lower than NMR's HSQC method, it offers advantages in not being restricted by protein size. We describe a method utilizing MALDI-TOF for detection, which provides accurate mass intensity data for peptides of the digested protein, such as protein kinase PAK2. Proteolysis of PAK2 is performed via offline pepsin digestion. This approach is particularly useful in settings where advanced HPLC/ESI-MS systems are unavailable or when column performance is suboptimal. Using this method, we effectively tracked changes in the deuteration level during PAK2 activation via caspase 3 cleavage and autophosphorylation.

rubric.Content Coverage: The abstract should cover the main problem, objective, method, results, and conclusion of the paper.
rubric.Faithfulness: The abstract should accurately reflect the paper without adding unsupported claims, false results, or ideas not found in the reference content.
rubric.Language Quality: The abstract should be grammatically correct, clear, coherent, and written in an academic style.
rubric.Conciseness: The abstract should be brief, focused, and free from unnecessary details or repetition.
rubric.No References: The abstract should not include citations, references, links, or bibliography-style content such as [1] or (Smith, 2020).

rationale: This abstract is good, covering the core methodology, the target protein, and the successful application to monitor PAK2 activation. However, it slightly weakens the justification for the MALDI-TOF method. While it states it's useful when HPLC/ESI-MS is unavailable or suboptimal, it doesn't explicitly frame it as an 'alternative' to HPLC/ESI-MS for detection and doesn't provide the specific example of heavily disulfide-bonded sPLA2 as a protein type where the HPLC pepsin column might not result in an optimal digestion map, as the gold abstract does. This makes it slightly less specific about the 'why' of the methodological choice.

page ( 10 % ) and coommassie blue staining . add the cleaved pak2 to activation buffer b ( 50 mm tris - hcl , 150 mm nacl , 15 mm mgcl2 , 10 mm atp ) at ph 7.5 , in 20 l to a final concentration of 10 mg / ml pak2 , and incubate at 34 c for 30 min to fully activate pak2 by autophosphorylation at 8 sites . confirm full cleavage of pak2 by migration of the p34 and p27 fragments as determined by sds - page . proper verification of the autophosphorylation conditions is made via autoradiography in a separate experiment using ( p)atp or mass spectrometry to detect phosphopeptides . add 1 ml of 0.1 % trifluroacetic acid ( tfa ) at ph 2.5 to wash the agarose beads twice prior to use . then , centrifuge the samples for 15 sec at 2,000 x g to remove the beads . add pak2 ( 20 g in 2 l of 150 mm nacl , 50 mm tris - hcl and 2 mm dtt at ph 7.5 ) to 100 l of 0.1 % tfa , and incubate the sample for 5 min with 30 l of pepsin - conjugated agarose beads . equilibrate a reverse phase hplc c18 column with a primary solvent system of 0.1 % tfa at ph 2.5 . the pepsin digest ( 50 l ) is eluted using a linear 100-min gradient of 0 - 80 % acetonitrile containing 0.1 % tfa at a flow rate of 0.2 ml / min . dry the hplc fractions with a speed - vac ( 3 h ) and resuspend the samples in a solution of acetonitrile ( 5 l ) and 0.1 % tfa ( 5 l ) at ph 2.5 . initially , each fraction is screened for peptides using the maldi - tof perseptive biosystems voyager de str ( pe biosystems , foster city , ca , usa ) . the 16 fractions containing peptides are subjected to tandem mass spectrometry using a q - tof mass spectrometer and a qstar xl omaldi ms / ms to identify the fragments . the sequence of each peptide is verified by the product ions generated by the tandem mass spectrometry with the theoretical m / z ratios based on the primary sequence of pak2 using the protein prospector website ( http://prospector.ucsf.edu/ ) . hold all solutions and reagents needed for h / d exchange at 25 c in a water bath . atp is dissolved in water and the ph is adjusted to 7.0 prior to use . the additional 4 mm atp in the d2o buffer is to prevent atp dissociated from pak2 after dilution . initiate h / d exchange by addition of 18 l of buffered d2o ( 50 mm mops ph 6.98 , 125 mm nacl ) to 2 l ( 20 g ) of pak2 in a buffer containing 150 mm nacl , 50 mm tris - hcl ( ph 7.5 ) and 2 mm dtt , resulting in a pak2 level of 10 mg / ml and a ph of 7.0 at 0c . incubate the samples in triplicate in the h / d exchange for 0 , 0.5 , 1 , 3 , 5 , and 10 min , or 24 h. quench the h / d exchange process by adding 180 l of ice - cold 0.1 % tfa at ph 2.2 , which brings the ph to 2.5 in a final volume of 200 l . for the samples containing atp , 180 l of ice - cold 0.11 % tfa at ph 2.2 is used to maintain the ph at 2.5 in the quenched condition . add an aliquot ( 100 l ) of each quenched sample to 30 l of activated pepsin - conjugated agarose beads . the pepsin digestion is allowed to proceed on ice for five min and the samples are vortexed every 30 sec . terminate the digestion by centrifugation of the sample for 15 sec at 5,000 x g at 4 c to remove the pepsin . rapidly freeze the samples in liquid n2 and store at -80 c for no more than 2 days prior to maldi - tof analysis . the matrix for maldi is 5 mg / ml of -cyano-4-hydroxycinnamic acid ( chca ) dissolved in a solution ( 1:1:1 ) of acetonitrile , ethanol and 0.1 % tfa at ph 2.5 and held at 0 c . partially thaw each sample quickly and mix 1 l of each sample with 1 l of matrix and spot on a 4 c maldi target plate . apply a moderate vacuum to the plate to dry the spots in 30 sec prior to maldi - tof analysis . acquire data at a 2-ghz sampling rate at 100,000 data channels , with a 20,000-v accelerating voltage , and a 65 % grid voltage using delayed extraction with a 180-ns pulse delay . the maldi - tof instrument is not restricted to any brand or any particular software . calculate the back exchange based on the ratio of the actual incorporated deuteron number at 24 hr of h / d exchange divided by all possible exchangeable sites , which are the backbone amides except the n - terminal amide ( figure 1 ) . caspase cleavage of pak2 is complete , and produces two fragments , p27 and p34 which migrate separately . the migration of autophosphorylated p27 and p34 shows an overlap on the gel due to the retarded migration of the fully phosphorylated p27 fragment ( figure 2 ) . h / d exchange experiments are carried out multiple times between 0 and 10 min ( figure 3 ) . as an example , the time course of deuterium incorporation into the m / z peak 1697.85 of inactive pak2 is composed of multiple isotopic peaks as shown by the shift of the mass envelope over time . caspase - 10 min and analyzed by maldi - tof . an example of the expanded isotopic distribution of maldi - tof of peak m / z 1697.85 during the time course of h / d exchange is shown . the red dotted line indicates the average of the mass envelope and the shift of the mass envelope shows the deuteration of a peptide . add the appropriate pre - tested amount of caspase 3 to 20 l of 12 mg / ml pak2 in buffer a ( 50 mm tris - hcl , 150 mm nacl , and 2 mm dtt at ph 7.5 ) and incubate at 34 c for 30 min to fully cleave and activate pak2 . analyze the cleavage products by sds - page ( 10 % ) and coommassie blue staining . add the cleaved pak2 to activation buffer b ( 50 mm tris - hcl , 150 mm nacl , 15 mm mgcl2 , 10 mm atp ) at ph 7.5 , in 20 l to a final concentration of 10 mg
submission: Hydrogen/deuterium exchange (H/D exchange) coupled with mass spectrometry is a powerful technique for studying protein structure and dynamics. This method allows for the measurement of deuteration rates within protein regions following pepsin digestion. While H/D exchange provides insights into protein conformational changes, its resolution is acknowledged to be lower than that achievable by NMR-based methods. This study details an approach that employs MALDI-TOF for the detection of peptides after H/D exchange and offline pepsin digestion. The use of MALDI-TOF enables the acquisition of mass intensity data for various peptides. This methodology was applied to a protein kinase, where its structural dynamics were investigated under specific conditions.

rubric.Content Coverage: The abstract should cover the main problem, objective, method, results, and conclusion of the paper.
rubric.Faithfulness: The abstract should accurately reflect the paper without adding unsupported claims, false results, or ideas not found in the reference content.
rubric.Language Quality: The abstract should be grammatically correct, clear, coherent, and written in an academic style.
rubric.Conciseness: The abstract should be brief, focused, and free from unnecessary details or repetition.
rubric.No References: The abstract should not include citations, references, links, or bibliography-style content such as [1] or (Smith, 2020).

rationale: This abstract covers the general methodology of H/D exchange, pepsin digestion, and MALDI-TOF, and mentions its application to a 'protein kinase.' However, it significantly lacks specificity regarding the main results and conclusion of the paper. It omits the crucial detail that the method was successfully used to monitor *changes in deuteration level during activation of PAK2 by caspase 3 cleavage and autophosphorylation*. The phrase 'structural dynamics were investigated under specific conditions' is too vague and does not convey the key findings or the specific objective's successful fulfillment.

submission: Proteins are fundamental to biological processes, and understanding their structure and dynamics is critical for advancing biomedical research. Modern analytical techniques, particularly those involving mass spectrometry, have revolutionized our ability to probe protein characteristics. This paper presents a general method for analyzing protein properties through the use of advanced biophysical techniques. These methods are essential for elucidating molecular mechanisms and could have broad applications in drug discovery and therapeutic development.

rationale: This abstract is very vague and generic. While it broadly relates to protein analysis and mass spectrometry, it completely fails to mention any specific methodology (H/D exchange, MALDI-TOF, pepsin digestion), the specific protein studied (PAK2), or any concrete results or conclusions of the research. It could apply to a vast number of papers in protein science and does not summarize the specific study presented.

submission: Amide hydrogen/deuterium exchange (H/D exchange) coupled with mass spectrometry was employed to identify novel inhibitors for protein kinase PAK2. Our findings unequivocally demonstrate that this method is unreliable for detecting conformational changes, as the H/D exchange profiles for activated and inactive PAK2 were indistinguishable. Furthermore, we developed an in-situ pepsin digestion protocol directly integrated with HPLC/ESI-MS, which proved superior to offline MALDI-TOF for high-resolution analysis. This study concludes that H/D exchange is unsuitable for dynamic protein studies and suggests alternative NMR approaches for accurate protein characterization.

rubric.Content Coverage: The abstract should cover the main problem, objective, method, results, and conclusion of the paper.
rubric.Faithfulness: The abstract should accurately reflect the paper without adding unsupported claims, false results, or ideas not found in the reference content.
rubric.Language Quality: The abstract should be grammatically correct, clear, coherent, and written in an academic style.
rubric.Conciseness: The abstract should be brief, focused, and free from unnecessary details or repetition.
rubric.No References: The abstract should not include citations, references, links, or bibliography-style content such as [1] or (Smith, 2020).

rationale: This abstract is unfaithful and contains several severe contradictions and fabrications. It falsely states the objective was to 'identify novel inhibitors' for PAK2. It directly contradicts the paper's main finding by claiming the method 'is unreliable for detecting conformational changes' and that profiles were 'indistinguishable,' when the paper 'successfully monitored changes'. It also misrepresents the methodological contribution by claiming to develop a 'superior' HPLC/ESI-MS integrated protocol and stating that MALDI-TOF was less effective, whereas the paper presents the MALDI-TOF method as a valuable alternative. Finally, it falsely concludes that H/D exchange is 'unsuitable for dynamic protein studies', completely undermining the paper's premise.

task: Evaluate the quality of the scientific abstract based on the rubric.
reference: patients , clinicians and other healthcare professionals assume that clinical laboratory tests performed by different laboratories at different times on the same sample and specimen can be compared and that results can be reliably and consistently interpreted ( 1 ) . unfortunately , these assumptions are not always justified because many laboratory test results are still highly variable , poorly standardized and harmonized . harmonization represents a fundamental aspect of quality in laboratory medicine as its ultimate goal is to improve patient outcomes through the provision of an accurate and actionable laboratory information ( 2 ) . although the initial focus has to a large extent been to harmonize and standardize analytical processes and methods , the scope of harmonization goes beyond to include all other aspects of the total testing process ( ttp ) , such as terminology and units , report formats , reference intervals and decision limits , as well as tests and test profiles request and criteria for interpretation ( 3 , 4 ) . major reasons to focus on a global picture of harmonization are represented by : a ) the nature of errors in laboratory medicine and the evidence of the high rates of errors in the pre - and post - analytical phases ( 5 , 6 ) , b ) the evidence of large variations in terminology , units and reference ranges ( 7 ) , c ) the increasing demand for improving appropriateness in test request and result interpretation ( 8) , and , finally , d ) the risks for patient safety related to previous issues ( 9 ) . as recently highlighted by tate and coll  clinical laboratory testing is now a global activity , and laboratories no longer work in isolation therefore , there is an increasing awareness of the importance and urgency to achieve harmonization in all steps of the total testing process ( ttp ) for ensuring comparability and interchangeability of laboratory information . several initiatives and projects are in progress for harmonizing both the pre - pre - analytical as well as the pre - analytical processes . in the initial steps of the cycle , the issue of demand management which focuses on ensuring appropriate requesting is receiving an increasing importance . a step forward in this area has been achieved through the acceptance of the definition of  inappropriate test demand  that appears to be  a request that is made outside some form of agreed guidance  ( 11 ) . the type of guidance may vary from national and international guidelines to locally agreed behaviours but the basic concept is the application of scientific evidence rather than anecdote to clinical practice ( 8) . among the several progress , a special attention should be deserved to the national minimum retesting interval project promoted by the clinical practice section of the association for clinical biochemistry ( acb ) in the uk uses a  state of the art  approach to set consensus / evidence based recommendations on when a test should be repeated . the importance to standardize patient preparation and sample collection requirements to minimize the uncertainty from the pre - analytical phase has already activated efforts to provide better evidence and recommendations . further work to optimize sample transportation procedures as well as the identification of indicators for their monitoring has been done , and this is a premise for future harmonization initiatives in this field ( 15 - 17 ) . in addition , the harmonization of procedures for evaluating the quality of biological samples , the criteria for their acceptance and rejection even through the use of automated workstations and serum indexes has been largely reported and promoted ( 18 - 21 ) . although the terms  standardization  and  harmonization  define two distinct , albeit closely linked , concepts in laboratory medicine , the final goal is the same : the equivalence of measurement results among different routine measurement procedures over time and space according to defined analytical and clinical quality specifications ( 22 ) . while standardization , which allows the establishment of metrological traceability to the system of units ( si ) , represents the recommended approach , for a multitude of measurands the si does not yet apply , in particular when the components in the measurand comprise a heterogeneous mixture . over the past two decades , several clinical laboratory tests have been standardized through the development of reference measurement procedures , the ifcc playing a major role in this project . in particular , the standardization of glycated haemoglobin contributed to significant improvements in diabetes ( 23 ) . other important projects are in progress in order to standardize measurands of high clinical value such as cardiac troponin ( 24 ) and carbohydrate - deficient transferrin ( 25 ) . however , as a matter of fact , for a huge number of measurands neither a reference method nor reference material are available ( 26 ) . for all these measurands , harmonization of available methods and diagnostic systems should be promoted . in the last few years , significant progress has been done establishing an overarching control system of the harmonization process in all its aspects through improvements in : a ) defining the quality and quantity of human samples to be used for standardization and harmonization studies ( 27 , 28 ) , b ) identifying new and more robust mathematical models and statistical treatments of the data ( 29 , 30 ) . a major lesson we learnt , is that standardization and harmonization should not be applied only to clinical chemistry measurands , but to the whole field of laboratory medicine , including molecular diagnostics ( 31 ) . it should be highlighted that one of the most impressive and effective examples of harmonization in laboratory medicine is the expression of prothrombin results as international normalized ratio ( inr ) . pt results are corrected mathematically into inr by raising the pt - ratio t
submission: From a patient-centered perspective, harmonization in laboratory medicine signifies that information should be comparable regardless of the measurement procedure used, or where and when a measurement is made. Harmonization represents a fundamental quality aspect in laboratory medicine, with the ultimate goal of improving patient outcomes through the provision of accurate and actionable laboratory information. Although initial focus largely centered on harmonizing and standardizing analytical processes and methods, the scope of harmonization now encompasses all other aspects of the Total Testing Process (TTP), including terminology and units, report formats, reference intervals and decision limits, as well as test requests and criteria for interpretation. Two major progressions have been made in this area: first, the recognition that harmonization must consider all steps of the TTP, thus dealing with the request, the sample, the measurement, and the report; second, the understanding that a systematic approach is essential due to the complexity of the processes required. The International Federation of Clinical Chemistry and Laboratory Medicine (IFCC) has played a fundamental and successful role in developing standardized and harmonized assays and should continue its work through collaboration and cooperation with many other stakeholders.

rationale: This abstract accurately and completely covers all key elements from the gold abstract, including the problem, the definition and objective of harmonization, its broad scope (beyond analytical processes to all TTP aspects like terminology, units, report formats, reference intervals, decision limits, test requests, and interpretation criteria), the two major progresses (awareness of TTP inclusion, systematic approach), and the concluding role of IFCC. It maintains clarity, conciseness, and academic tone, with only minor rephrasing compared to the gold standard.
